title: lithocardium thibaudianum
intro: Lithocardium thibaudianum, also known as Digoxin, is a cardiac glycoside extracted from the foxglove plant (Digitalis purpurea) and used as a medicinal herb. The plant has been traditionally used to treat heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, due to its ability to increase the force of heart contractions. The medicinal properties of Lithocardium thibaudianum include its ability to slow heart rate and increase cardiac output, providing health benefits such as improved cardiovascular function, reduced symptoms of heart failure, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Lithocardium thibaudianum is often prepared as a tincture or pill for oral consumption, and its medicinal properties are typically extracted through a process of solvent extraction.
